context.meal: buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing
context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop in Front

episode_text:
day=6; meal=buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing; setting=Working on laptop with laptop in Front; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is fruit AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to reheat a cold fruit meal, consistent with fruit being preferred unheated. IF food_condition is warm savory food such as bite-sized sandwiches or chicken with hash brown AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the right or social with a person in front THEN 1 min or 2 min, with 2 min as the stronger general tendency, BECAUSE the user has preferred moderate reheating for non-fruit meals. IF food_condition is hot savory food with sauce or dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is working on a laptop in front THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user explicitly chose stronger reheating in that front-focused work context.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the view toward a front-facing social partner. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve the front view while working. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve the view toward a right-side social partner. IF dining context is watching TV with the TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the view toward the TV on the right.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person in front or on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken with hash brown or fruit THEN medium BECAUSE the user explicitly accepted a moderate pace in those social meals. IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches THEN medium BECAUSE the user repeatedly accepted a moderate pace there. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is buffalo chicken bites with ranch and potato wedges THEN medium BECAUSE the user also selected a moderate pace in that front-focused work context.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken strips, or buffalo chicken bites AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the right, social with a person in front, or working on a laptop in front THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the narrower-bite option, which is compatible with limited mouth opening. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, or buffalo chicken bites with ranch AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the right, social with a person in front, or working on a laptop in front THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred explicit confirmation of bite acquisition rather than skipping verification. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF user has limited trunk control and cannot lean forward AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food type observed so far THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE outside-mouth transfer is poorly matched to the user's physical abilities.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward, making inside-mouth transfer the physically compatible mode.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is watching TV on the right, social with a person in front, or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected LED-only signaling across multiple front- and right-attention contexts. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is social with a person in front or on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and avoids unreliable open-mouth signaling, especially in social interaction. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable low-ambiguity readiness method in attention-demanding contexts.

IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is social with a person in front or on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and avoids mouth-based readiness detection that may be unreliable for this user. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable low-ambiguity readiness method.

IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is social with a person in front or on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and more reliable than mouth-based signaling. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable readiness method.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is watching TV on the right, social with a person in front, or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected LED-only completion signaling across observed contexts. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food type observed so far THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently accepted the reliable low-effort completion method for bites across observed contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND user cannot reliably perform head gestures AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE perception for drinking depends on head-nod gestures, which are not physically reliable for this user.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND user cannot reliably perform head gestures AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE perception for wiping depends on head-nod gestures, which are not physically reliable for this user.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred retracting in front-facing social meals to reduce prolonged visual obstruction. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred retracting in a right-side social setting, indicating a broader tendency to reduce obstruction during social meals. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is buffalo chicken bites with ranch and potato wedges THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred retracting while engaged with a front-facing task, consistent with wanting to keep the workspace visible. IF dining context is watching TV with the TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose not to retract in that entertainment context despite caring about right-side occlusion, indicating a preference to avoid extra meal time there.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user accepted some dipping while limiting messiness in a social context. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user again preferred limited dipping, consistent with reducing messiness during an attention-demanding front-focused activity. IF food has no dip or sauce available AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context so far THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for non-dippable meals, including fruit, sandwiches without dip use, and chicken with hash brown.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is watching TV on the right, social with a person in front, or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the longest wait, reinforcing a stable preference to avoid automatic progression without ample time to intervene. No context-dependent variation observed yet.
